heroHeadline: Day 37: O3 Collective Council Structure Hardens; Operativo Enjambre Institutionalizes Anti-Corruption Framework
heroSubtitle: Day 37 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco. O3 (Juan Carlos Valencia González) continues cementing control under a collective council structure, leveraging Grupo Élite's paramilitary capacity to enforce organizational cohesion without a single exposed figurehead. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) — carrying a $5 million U.S. State Department bounty — remains the primary unresolved high-value targeting objective of the post-Tapalpa campaign. Operativo Enjambre, after producing dozens of CJNG-linked official arrests across Jalisco and Michoacán, is transitioning from reactive sweeps to a structured anti-corruption enforcement framework. Argentina's March 26 FTO designation is now in full effect, extending the multilateral legal coalition against CJNG beyond its 40-country footprint. FIFA World Cup 2026 Guadalajara security deployment of 100,000 personnel remains in planning phase.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Wall Street Journal, Al Jazeera, UPI, Excélsior, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, Janes, Chatham House, WOLA, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: March 31, 2026.
